## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-23 05:07:45 PT

Found 0 violations across 2 files. All code changes comply with dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL exception handling - Compliant (proper use of early continue and explicit error boundaries)
✅ Exception usage - Compliant (RuntimeError appropriately raised at error boundary with detailed context)
✅ Variable declaration - Compliant (variables declared close to use)
✅ Code simplification - Compliant (test refactoring removed unnecessary call counting)
✅ Indentation depth - Compliant (maximum 2 levels in new code)
✅ Absolute imports - Compliant
✅ Error messages - Compliant (detailed, actionable error message for skipped/cancelled runs)

### Violations Summary
None

### Files Reviewed
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py`: 0 violations (good refactoring with early continue and clear error boundary)
- `tests/integration/test_real_github.py`: 0 violations (test simplified by removing unnecessary state tracking)

### Positive Observations
- **Early continue pattern**: The refactored code checks distinct_id match first, then validates conclusion - this is clearer than the previous nested structure
- **Clear error boundary**: RuntimeError with detailed message provides actionable feedback about workflow configuration issues
- **Test simplification**: Removed `call_count` tracking variable that was only used for assertions, not actual behavior verification

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-02-23 05:07:45 PT**: No violations detected. Code demonstrates good LBYL patterns, appropriate error boundaries, and effective test simplification.
- **2026-02-23 04:50:01 PT**: Found 1 critical issue (triple definition of `claude_ci` function in admin.py — appears to be merge conflict)

</details>

✅ `@patch|monkeypatch\.` - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches

✅ **modifying business logic without adding a test** - Compliant. Test was updated from test_trigger_workflow_skips_cancelled_runs to test_trigger_workflow_raises_on_skipped_cancelled_runs

None

### Files Reviewed
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/integration/test_real_github.py`: 0 violations

---

<!-- test-coverage-review -->

## ✅ Test Coverage Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-23 05:07:22 PT

No violations detected. All source modifications have corresponding test coverage.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Source Files Reviewed

| File | Status | Tests |
|------|--------|-------|
| `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py` | Modified | ✅ `tests/integration/test_real_github.py` |

### Patterns Checked

✅ **Untested source additions** - None found
✅ **Untested source modifications** - None found
✅ **Net test reduction** - No reduction detected

### Summary

The modified `trigger_workflow()` method now raises `RuntimeError` when a matched workflow run is skipped/cancelled instead of continuing to poll. The test `test_trigger_workflow_raises_on_skipped_cancelled_runs()` provides comprehensive coverage of this behavioral change, including:
- Workflow trigger via mocked subprocess
- Matching run detection by distinct_id
- Assertion that `RuntimeError` is raised for skipped runs

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-02-23 05:07:22 PT**: Modified `trigger_workflow()` logic covered by updated test. Changed behavior to raise immediately on skipped/cancelled runs; test renamed and refactored to verify new behavior.
- **2026-02-23 04:41:34 PT**: All source files have corresponding tests. 17 source modifications covered by 10 test files with 0 violations.

</details>

comments.body:
<!-- dignified-python-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-02-23 05:07:32 PT

No violations found. All Python changes adhere to dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL (Look Before You Leap) - Condition checks before processing
✅ Exception handling - Proper boundaries with informative error messages
✅ Path operations - Pathlib only, proper .get() usage for dict access
✅ Imports - Module-level, absolute imports
✅ Function signatures - Proper parameter organization
✅ Error patterns - Clear, user-friendly error messages with context

### Code Changes Summary

**packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py** (lines 383-400)
- Refactored workflow run lookup logic to use early-exit pattern
- Added explicit RuntimeError when matched run is skipped/cancelled (proper error boundary)
- Improved error messaging for debugging workflow failures
- LBYL pattern: checks condition before processing, continues early on mismatches

**tests/integration/test_real_github.py** (lines 628-673)
- Updated test to verify new exception behavior
- Simplified mock setup (removed unnecessary call_count tracking)
- Uses pytest.raises() to validate RuntimeError is raised with correct message

### Files Reviewed
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py`: ✅ Compliant
- `tests/integration/test_real_github.py`: ✅ Compliant

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-02-23 05:07:32 PT**: Reviewed refactored workflow run lookup logic - LBYL pattern, proper exception boundary, clear error messages
- **2026-02-23 04:50:51 PT**: All patterns compliant - no violations detected

</details>
